what_it_does: Defines a single ATTACHMENT_TYPE dict declaring a 'recall' attachment type (label 'Recall scope'): the channel-scoped recall selector that cc_channel op=recall runs over, described as the §3 manifest recall_scope and the '5th wire'; target_kind='scope' (a project/session/segment selector, not an address), multi=False.
observations: target_kind is 'scope' — explicitly a recall-scope selector (project/session/segment), not an address.
multi=False — unlike the other five types in this batch, only one recall scope per channel.
Ties to cc_channel op=recall as the consumer.
References both the §3 manifest recall_scope and a '5th wire' seam concept.
Label differs from a simple capitalization of id ('Recall scope').
standouts: The only multi=False type in this batch — a channel has exactly one recall scope.
Names the consuming operation explicitly (cc_channel op=recall) and the '5th wire' seam.
conventions: Same single-dict descriptor pattern as siblings.
Breaks the multi=True norm seen in the other batch members (deliberately single).
Inline comment clarifies scope means a selector, not an address.
notes: Registry entry for the 'recall' type: a single channel-scoped recall selector that cc_channel op=recall runs over (the 5th wire).
questions: What are the four other wires the '5th wire' joins, and how is a project/session/segment selector encoded?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the recall-scope selector type as the docstring states.
intention_signals: Aims to give a channel a single recall scope selector so a channel-level recall operation can run over the right project/session/segment slice.
novelty: A scope-selector (not address) target plus the single-cardinality '5th wire' recall framing.
contribution: Contributes the declarative definition of the recall-scope attachment type, its selector-not-address semantics, single cardinality, and link to cc_channel op=recall.
summary_for_embedding: A descriptor module declaring the 'recall' channel attachment type: a single recall-scope selector (project/session/segment) that cc_channel op=recall runs over, multi=False.
intention_for_embedding: To register a channel's single recall scope selector as a typed attachment driving channel-level recall (the 5th wire).
